description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rugged, natural appearance. Its surface is uneven, featuring subtle undulations and a mottled texture that suggests it has been weathered over time. The coloration is predominantly a muted, earthy brown with some darker and lighter patches, giving it a realistic, organic look. The stone is asymmetrical, with one side appearing broader and flatter, while the opposite side is more narrow and elongated. There are no visible carvings, inscriptions, or artificial modifications, indicating it is likely a naturally occurring rock. Its size and form make it suitable as an obstacle or decorative element in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas.
description: A rugged, irregularly shaped brown stone with a mottled, weathered surface and natural, asymmetrical contours.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and rounded, with a rough, uneven surface and subtle color variations.
description_view_1: This side is narrow and elongated, showing the stone's thinnest profile and highlighting its vertical ridges.
description_view_2: The stone looks wide and slightly flattened, with visible surface undulations and mottled brown tones.
description_view_3: This view reveals a tall, tapering side with pronounced vertical lines and a more slender silhouette.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and irregular color patches with no regular or artificial patterning.
attribute_weathering: Surface appears rough and naturally weathered, with no significant cracks or biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural landscaping elements or contrasts with paved surfaces, depending on placement.
